title: hepatica asiatica subsp. japonica
intro: Hepatica asiatica subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese liverleaf, is a plant species native to East Asia that belongs to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). Traditionally, the plant's roots and rhizomes are used in herbal medicine, containing compounds such as iridoid glycosides and flavonoids, which are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Japanese liverleaf include its potential to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever reduction, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or decoctions, while the plant's leaves can be used fresh or dried in salads and herbal remedies.
